description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a dark green fuselage and wings, featuring roundels on the upper and lower wings, likely indicating military or historical use. The aircraft has two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically, connected by struts and wires, and a single open cockpit situated just behind the upper wing. The nose houses a visible piston engine with a two-bladed propeller, and the landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid. The tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer with a tricolor rudder and horizontal stabilizers. The surface appears to be primarily metal with some fabric-covered sections, typical of early 20th-century aircraft. Markings and insignia on the fuselage and tail add to its period-accurate appearance.
description: A vintage green biplane with roundels, an open cockpit, and a visible piston engine with a two-bladed propeller. It features dual stacked wings, a tricolor rudder, and fixed landing gear.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the two-bladed propeller, engine cowling, and upper wing roundels.
description_view_1: Left-rear view highlighting the open cockpit, tricolor rudder, and side fuselage markings.
description_view_2: Rear-left view focusing on the tail section, horizontal stabilizers, and the stacked wing structure.
description_view_3: Front-right view emphasizing the propeller, landing gear, and cockpit placement behind the upper wing.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-bladed propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers; tricolor rudder.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward-mounted on the fuselage.
